title: Customs and Excise (Persons with Disabilities) (Remission) Regulations, 2021

sections.heading: (1) These Regulations may be cited as the Customs and
sections.text:
Excise (Persons with Disabilities) (Remission) Regulations, 2021.
(2) These Regulations shall come into operation on 1st January,

sections.heading: 2. In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.text:
“modified vehicle” means a non-conventional motor vehicle
specifically altered or adapted prior to importation to suit
the disability of the importer;
“person with disability” has the meaning assigned to the words Act No. 6 of
in the Persons with Disabilities Act, 2012; 2012
“Zambia Agency for Persons with Disabilities” means the
Zambia Agency for Persons with Disabilities continued
under the Persons with Disabilities Act, 2012; and Act No. 6 of
2012
“Zambia Revenue Authority” means the Zambia Revenue
Authority established under the Zambia Revenue Authority
Act. Cap. 321

sections.heading: (1) A person with disability who is registered with the Zambia Application
sections.text:
Agency for Persons with Disabilities may apply to the Minister for for
remission of duty paid or payable in respect of one modified motor remission
vehicle in Form I set out in the Schedule.
Copies of this Statutory Instrument can be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka. Price K 16.00 each.
752 Statutory Instruments 31st December, 2021
(2) An application referred to under subregulation (1) shall be
accompanied by—
(a) Tax Payer Identification Number (TPIN) Certificate issued
by the Zambia Revenue Authority;
(b) certificate of registration issued by the Zambia Agency
for Persons with Disabilities;
(c) recommendation letter from the ministry responsible for
persons with disabilities;
(d) invoice for the purchase of the modified motor vehicle;
(e) the applicant’s bank statements for the last twelve months
immediately preceding the application; and
(f) proof of donation where the modified motor vehicle is
donated.
Request for 4. The Minister may request an applicant to submit additional
additional information in relation to an application in Form II set out in the
information Schedule.

sections.heading: (1) The Minister shall, on the recommendation by the
sections.text:
Grant of
application ministry responsible for persons with disabilities, grant an application
for remission for remission of duty paid or payable to an applicant referred to in
regulation 3(1), where the application meets the requirements under
these Regulations.
(2) The Minister shall, where the Minister grants a remission
of duty, inform the applicant in Form III set out in the Schedule.
(3) A remission under this regulation shall be granted to an
applicant—
(a) only once in a period of five years; and
(b) on condition that the modified motor vehicle concerned is
not sold, or otherwise disposed of, except with the prior
consent of the Commissioner- General and on payment
of duty, if any, at the rate leviable at the date of that sale
or disposal.
(4) For the purpose of determining the amount of duty payable
on the modified motor vehicle under subregulation (3)(b), the
Commissioner-General may take into consideration the depreciation
of the modified motor vehicle from the date of its importation or
removal from bond and shall remit the duty if the sale or disposal
is effected after five years of the date when the remission of duty
was granted.

sections.heading: (1) The Minister shall reject an application for remission
sections.text:
Rejection of
application if—
(a) the applicant fails to meet the requirements specified in
these Regulations; or
31st December, 2021 Statutory Instruments 753
(b) the applicant submits false information in relation to the
application.
(2) The Minister shall, where the Minister rejects an application
under this regulation, inform the applicant of the rejection within
thirty days of receipt of an application in Form IV set out in the
Schedule.
754 Statutory Instruments 31st December, 2021
